positive_description: Left-lateralized upper face movement — subtle left brow lift and left periorbital widening. The left side of the upper face opens slightly while the right remains comparatively neutral. Creates a mild left-biased quizzical or skeptical look.
negative_description: Left-lateralized upper face depression — the left brow area lowers subtly and the left periorbital region narrows. A very mild left-sided furrowing or closing quality.
symmetry_notes: Asymmetric — this is the left-sided member of the psi14/psi15 conjugate pair. The 3/4 views show different deformation magnitudes between left and right sides, with the left side showing more activity. Follows the same conjugate pair pattern as psi11/psi12 but in the upper face.
artifact_notes: No mesh artifacts at +/-3.0. Very subtle deformation. Safe at higher weights than primary components.
primary_face_region: left brow, left periorbital area, left upper face
useful_for: bilateral brow expression (as conjugate pair with psi15)

interactions.psi15: CONJUGATE PAIR. psi14+psi15 at equal weights produces bilateral brow/upper face expression. Mirror of the psi11/psi12 relationship but for the upper face.
interactions.psi2: psi2 drives dramatic upper face/mouth changes symmetrically; psi14 provides fine asymmetric upper face control
interactions.psi11: Same conjugate architecture — psi11/12 for mouth, psi14/15 for brow region

prose: Psi14 is the left-sided member of the second documented conjugate pair (psi14/psi15), operating in the upper face. Where psi11/psi12 lateralize mouth corner movement, psi14/psi15 lateralize brow and periorbital movement. Alone, psi14 produces a subtle left-side brow lift and eye-area widening — a half-skeptical, half-quizzical quality limited to the left upper face. Paired with psi15 at equal weight, the two halves would combine into a bilateral upper-face expression. The deformations are subtle at the tested weights, consistent with mid-frequency PCA components. This pair has not yet been deployed in any expression recipe.
